hook: You already know how to compare numbers — like noticing that a 12-pack of soda costs less per can than a 6-pack. What you might not realize is that this everyday thinking is the foundation of one of the most powerful tools in mathematics. In this unit, you'll discover how ratios and proportions quietly run the world around you, from sale prices to recipe conversions to calculating a tip at a restaurant.
concept: Every topic in this unit is built on the same core idea: comparing two quantities in a meaningful way and using that relationship to solve real problems. Formally, this is the study of *ratios, proportions, and percent* — and once you see the connection, everything clicks together.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to write and interpret ratios and rates as meaningful comparisons between two quantities, like miles per hour or boys to girls in a class.
Master simplifying any rate down to a 'per one' unit rate so you can quickly compare deals, speeds, and prices side by side.
Apply cross multiplication to solve proportions and find unknown values whenever two ratios are set equal, such as scaling a recipe or reading a map.
You will learn to see percent as a special ratio 'out of 100,' connecting it directly to fractions and decimals you already know.
Calculate any percent of a number confidently by translating the percent into a multiplication problem, like finding 35% of 80.
You will learn to measure how much a value has grown or shrunk by computing percent change using the formula $\frac{\text{new} - \text{original}}{\text{original}} \times 100$.
Apply everything you've learned about percents to real-world money situations — calculating sales tax, leaving a tip, and finding the final price after a discount.
